'''Paul Marquet''' (born 26 September 1969 in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]]) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played primarily as a {{rlp|sr}} for Australian clubs the [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Hunter Mariners]] and [[Melbourne Storm]], and for English club, the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early life==
Born in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]]. Paul played junior rugby league for [[Raymond Terrace, New South Wales]].

==Playing career==
A resilient {{rlp|sr}} forward, Marquet has the honour of scoring the first try for the [[Hunter Mariners]] in their first game of the [[1997 Super League (Australia) season|1997 Super League season]]. He would also score the first try for the [[Melbourne Storm]] on their first home game of the [[1998 NRL season]] against the [[North Sydney Bears]].<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Cadigan |first1=Neil |title=Pepe's re-birth |journal=[[Big League]] |date=22 April 1998 |volume=79 |issue=8 |pages=14–15 |publisher=Pacific Publications Pty Ltd |location=Sydney, New South Wales}}</ref>

Marquet participated in seven finals series and two [[NRL Grand Final]]s, the most memorable being his appearance as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[1999 NRL Grand Final]], played on his 30th birthday.<ref>{{cite news|format=fee required|newspaper=Daily Post|title=Marquet will cut up rough Rugby League Carpenter sights Saints|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-2916507.html|accessdate=2007-11-16| date=2002-02-23}}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> Having won the 1999 Premiership, the [[Melbourne Storm]] traveled to England to contest the [[2000 World Club Challenge]] against [[Super League]] Champions [[St Helens R.F.C.]], with Marquet again starting as a {{rlp|sr}}er in the victory. He played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] from the interchange bench in their [[2001 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Parramatta Eels]].

===Highlights===
* ''First Grade Debut:'' 
: 1990 – Round 1, Newcastle Knights vs [[North Sydney Bears]] at [[EnergyAustralia Stadium]], 18 March<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Sports Data|title=NRL Stats Paul Marquet|url=http://www.nrlstats.com/archive/players.cfm?PlayerID=410|accessdate=2007-11-16|archive-date=28 July 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080728011424/http://www.nrlstats.com/archive/players.cfm?PlayerID=410|url-status=dead}}</ref>
* ''Premierships:'' (2)
: [[1999 NRL Grand Final|1999 – Melbourne Storm]]
: [[2001 NRL Grand Final|2001 – Newcastle Knights]]
* ''World Club Challenge Champions:'' (1)
: [[2000 World Club Challenge|2000 – Melbourne Storm]]
